Case Study — Email Creative Refresh

This case study documents a creative refresh initiative I led for TELUS Mobility's cross-sell email program. Faced with a multi-quarter decline in conversion rate (CVR dropping to 0.23%) and rising unsubscribe rates, I identified the root cause as stale creative and generic messaging through primary market research, and collaborated with our creative agency to redesign the email experience from the ground up. The result was a 58% drop in unsubscribe rate, a 47% lift in conversion, and a 2x increase in CTR — currently tracking 1K activations ahead of target.

Problem Statement
Stagnant email creative and generic offer messaging drove CVR down to 0.23% (declining quarter over quarter), unsubscribe rates up to 0.12%, and conversion down 24% — signalling customers were actively disengaging from communications.
Offer Module — Before

The original email lacked visual hierarchy and offer differentiation. A flat, text-heavy layout with no clear focal point reduced scroll depth and limited engagement.

RTB Module — Before

The secondary RTB module was generic and disconnected from the offer, failing to reinforce why the product was relevant and missing the opportunity to build urgency or emotional resonance.

Original offer email — generic messaging, no scroll incentive
Original RTB module — generic bundling message, no offer connection
Insights from Market Research
🔍

Customers were not scrolling past the offer module, and the secondary RTB module failed to reinforce the offer's unique value proposition — creating a disconnect between what was being sold and why it mattered. Primary research including quantitative surveys and qualitative focus groups confirmed that offer fatigue and lack of personalisation were the primary drivers of disengagement.

A/B Testing Methodology
Before scaling, I ran a structured A/B test comparing the legacy creative against the redesigned version across a controlled audience segment. The test isolated two key variables — the offer module visual and the RTB module messaging — while holding send timing, subject line, and audience segment constant. Statistical significance was confirmed before rolling out to the full email journey.
Campaign Evolution — Before and After
I joined the team in Q4 2025. The table below shows the direct impact of introducing BTL personalisation and offer segmentation on campaign performance.
Metric Q1–Q3 2025 (Avg)
Before joining		 Nov/Dec 2025
First campaign cycle		 Q1 2026
Scaled rollout		 Change
CVR 0.23% (-0.04% YoY) 0.33% 0.27% +0.10% peak
Orders 9.6K (-0.4K YoY) 13.1K 15.6K +62% YoY
DNC Rate 0.12% 0.06% 0.05% -58%
Segmentation Region + language Priority vs BAU Lifestyle-based (traveller, tech enthusiast, price sensitive) Added BTL tiers
Shaded columns reflect periods after joining and introducing BTL personalisation and offer segmentation.
Solution
Offer Module — Redesigned

Introduced visual momentum through bold colour, TELUS-branded critters, and a limited-time offer banner to create urgency and encourage scrolling. Expanded the offer beyond rate plans to include a flagship device launch, broadening appeal across customer segments.

RTB Module — Redesigned

Rebuilt to mirror the offer module's visual language and speak directly to the specific offer's value. Added a rotating GIF to drive dwell time and sustained engagement. Together, the modules create a cohesive, scrollable experience guiding the customer from awareness to action.

Redesigned offer module — TELUS critter, bold colour, clear CTA
Flagship device integration — diversifying offer beyond rate plans
Animated GIF module — drives dwell time and sustained engagement
RTB module redesigned — tailored to offer, visual language aligned
Results
Impact After Creative Refresh and Scaling
2x
CTR increase in A/B test vs legacy creative
58%
Decrease in unsubscribe rate (0.12% to 0.05%)
47%
Increase in conversion once scaled
+62%
YoY order growth (9.6K to 15.6K)
A/B testing validated the redesign before scaling. Once rolled out across the full email journey, the initiative reversed a multi-quarter CVR decline and is currently tracking 1K activations ahead of target.
A/B Test Creative Variants
Multiple creative variants were tested across different offer positioning, animal mascots, price presentation styles, and colour treatments to identify the highest-performing combination before scaling to the full audience.
Offer positioning variants — testing price presentation and layout hierarchy
Animal mascot variants — identifying which critter drove highest engagement
